body:
# Passion Fit course QA — which module reports Pete has sent, and their state

## SESSION STATE 20 Jul (goal: execute converged plan to 100%)
- Phase 1 (bug reporter) BUILT + VERIFIED live (fa15c0b). Both test reports
  resolved. KNOWN MINOR: admin nav open-count badge didn't visibly render with
  2 open — check src/app/admin/admin-nav.tsx badge injection next session.
- Phase 2 (member-data audit) DONE. 22 confirmed findings. 11 shipped
  (e682add + 33bc9fa via 6c1d30e), incl. 2 live security fixes (TRUNCATE
  revoke, forum un-delete). 4 data decisions on CC page /m/pf-md-decisions:
  (1) 112 blank-history completions — recommend backfill; (2) 13 evidence-less
  completions + server gate; (3) dead notifications bell; (4) drop video_progress
  + the two session backup tables. AWAITING PETE on those 4.
  Reverted one fix (detail auto-append removal) — would lock existing learners
  out; it's now part of decision 1's remedy.
- CMS parity audit: 27/33 fixed (3870ad7, aa7e04c); 6 lows accepted/deferred.

**PETE READS IT HERE: commandcentre.info/m/pf-course-qa** (CC page `pf-course-qa`,
section Passion Fit / Internal, tier private — anonymous requests correctly 404,
same as the existing `pf-concepts` page).
To update: rewrite `module_content.html` for module_key `pf-course-qa` via
`cc-sql.py`. It is pure data — NO deploy needed. Source HTML kept at
`scratchpad/cc-pf-qa.html`.
Secondary copy (Claude artifact, Pete could not open it locally):
https://claude.ai/code/artifact/324cf9f1-f445-4843-ba16-1b5dda7c8d1f
Redeploy it by republishing `scratchpad/pf-qa-status.html`, or pass that URL as
`url` from a new conversation. Keep it current when a report lands — it is the
thing that stops Pete re-sending reports he has already had actioned.

Pete is working through the course module by module and sending detailed QA
reports. He has re-sent the same report more than once because there was no
record of what had already been handled. **Check this note before asking him
anything about a module report, and update it when a new report lands.**

## Reports received

| Module | Report received | State |
|---|---|---|
| 1 — Effective Goal Setting | yes | actioned |
| 2 — Commitment Continuum | yes | actioned (he retracted the reflection-persistence claim himself) |
| 3 — Prioritisation | yes | actioned (INTERACTIVE_TOOL marker, duplicate prompts) |
| 4 — Control the Controllables | yes | actioned |
| 5 — Transactional State | yes | actioned; the REFLECTION: claim was already stale when sent |
| 6 — Direction/Support Matrix | yes, **sent 3 times** | actioned, see below |
| 7 — Intuition Scale & Learning Behaviours | yes | actioned, see below |
| Category 3 intro | yes | no bugs found by Pete; nothing to do |
| 8 — High Functioning Matrix | yes | actioned, see below |
| 9 — The Development Paradox | yes | actioned, see below |

## Module 8 — item-by-item state (verified 19 Jul 2026)

- **Nav tick disagreed with the Confirm gate** — FIXED (commit a27e2ac).
  Verified cause: `ProgressTracker` fires on MOUNT, so merely opening a tab
  appended that stage to `completed_stages`, which drove the tick; the Confirm
  page meanwhile checks for real evidence rows. Ticks for quiz/reflection/tool
  now come from the same evidence. Visiting still unlocks the next section so
  nobody is locked out. Measured blast radius at the time: 4 of 170
  `concept_progress` rows had a tick with no evidence (quiz 1 — Pete's own,
  since self-resolved by his re-submission; reflection 3; tool 1).
- **KNOWN WEAKNESS, not yet raised with Pete:** the Confirm gate's `stepsDone`
  reads `completed_stages` contains 'detail', and `ProgressTracker` appends
  'detail' on merely opening the Steps tab. So the Steps requirement is
  satisfied by visiting, and the "Mark complete" button is effectively
  decorative. Tightening it would force learners to press Mark complete, which
  is a behaviour change Pete has not asked for. Raise before changing.
- **Tool design (4 sliders vs 2x2 plot)** — Pete flagged as a design suggestion,
  NOT a defect, and has not green-lit a change. Converges with the separate
  finding that this tool's config contradicts its own module (item 5 below).
  Treat as ONE decision, not two.

## Module 7 — item-by-item state (verified 19 Jul 2026)

- **Blurred step-1 hero image** — FIXED (commit 1471206). Was DELIBERATE
  (`opacity-20 blur-sm` on `isFirst`, inherited from the old platform as a
  "teaser"). Two independent QA passes read it as a broken/stuck image, so the
  treatment was removed. If Pete ever wants the faded look back, that is the
  line to restore. Supersedes the earlier "awaiting Pete's call" item.
- **Quiz phantom pre-selected option** — HARDENED, not root-caused (1471206).
  Pete's diagnosis ("survives reload, so it's read from stored data") is
  DISPROVED: his own `quiz_progress` row for this module is `{}` / position 0,
  so there was nothing stored to restore. Other members' stored answers are
  varied (0,2,3,2,1 etc), not a constant last-index. Fix applied: the Radix
  `RadioGroup` had no `key`, so one instance was reused across questions that
  all share option values "0".."3". Now keyed to `q.id`. NOT reproduced
  directly — ask Pete to re-test before calling it closed.
- **"Tool tab missing / inconsistent"** — NOT A BUG. Verified across all 13
  active modules: exactly 4 have a tool stage AND a tool config
  (commitment-level, direction-support-matrix, effective-goal-setting,
  high-functioning-matrix); the other 9 have neither. Zero mismatches. Pete's
  claim that Modules 3 and 4 show a Tool tab without a configured tool is
  incorrect. Only real quirk: `/concept/{id}/tool` is still reachable by direct
  URL on tool-less modules and renders a graceful placeholder. Harmless.

## Module 6 — item-by-item state (verified 19 Jul 2026)

- **Tool quadrant/dot mismatch** — FIXED, live. Classification split moved to
  5.5, the true midpoint of the 1-10 scale, matching the dot formula
  `((v-1)/9)*100%`. Commit e8869c6.
- **INTERACTIVE_QUADRANTS** — removed. Zero `INTERACTIVE` strings remain in
  `cms_content_blocks`. NOTE: there is no `INTERACTIVE_*` template processor in
  the codebase at all — these are dead text from the old-platform import, not a
  failing renderer. Pete's "systemic processor bug" theory is wrong, gently.
- **`- ` bullets** — FIXED, live. `RichParagraphs` now groups dashed lines into
  a real `<ul>`.
- **Quiz answers not retained** — FIXED, live. Progress saved on the old
  platform keys answers by POSITION; the runner reads by question id. 31 saved
  quizzes affected. Reader now accepts both formats. No member data altered.
- **"Paul Hersy"** — corrected to Hersey.
- **Stronger completion gating (his point 5)** — already universal. All 13
  active modules have a quiz stage AND quiz questions, so the evidence gate
  applies everywhere. What he saw in Category 1 predates that fix.

## OPEN — waiting on Pete, do not re-ask more than once per session

1. **Step 3 is now empty.** Deleting the INTERACTIVE_QUADRANTS placeholder
   removed the symptom but left the heading "The Four Quadrants &
   Considerations" with zero content blocks beneath it. **RESOLVED 19 Jul 2026.**
   Recovered Passion Fit's ORIGINAL wording from the live Lovable bundle at
   `https://befabulous.me/assets/index-nLneSmph.js` — the old app DID have a
   renderer (`if (v.startsWith("INTERACTIVE_QUADRANTS")) return <Rfe/>`), and the
   four quadrant objects sit immediately before that component in the bundle.
   Restored as four `callout` blocks at display_order 6-9, matching the callout
   pattern already used for quadrants in high-functioning-matrix. Existing blocks
   from 7 up were shifted +3; verified 45 blocks / 45 distinct orders.
   **Technique worth reusing: when content looks lost, the old Lovable bundle is
   still served at befabulous.me and contains the original strings.**

   DONE 19 Jul 2026: `cms_tool_configs` for direction-support-matrix held GENERIC
   filler ("you just need cheerleading") contradicting the module's coachability
   framing. Pete approved replacing it; the four quadrants now carry the real
   text (title = axes, description = coachability rating, recommendation = the
   full explanation, colours matching the component's own defaults). Verified all
   four keys match the component's lookups and zero filler strings remain.

5. **NEW — high-functioning-matrix tool contradicts its own module.** Its
   `cms_tool_configs` describes axes "Context awareness" vs "Self-regulation"
   with quadrants Reactive / Aware / Disciplined / High functioning. The MODULE
   teaches Mindset/Attitude vs Environment producing HIGH FUNCTIONING /
   RESILIENCE / SELF-SABOTAGE / LOW FUNCTIONING (callouts at display_order
   8/11/14/17). Completely different model — same class of filler as DSM was.
   NOT a simple text swap: `HighFunctioningMatrix.tsx` is not a 2x2 plot, it
   renders one 1-10 slider per dimension, so renaming the quadrants changes what
   learners are asked to score. Needs Pete's steer on the instrument, not just
   the wording. Only 2 saved results exist for this tool.
2. **Step 1 blurred image.** `opacity-20 blur-sm` on `isFirst` is DELIBERATE —
   the code calls it a faded teaser. Not a bug. Pete read it as broken, which is
   itself the signal. Awaiting his call on whether to make it sharp.
3. ~~47 of 49 saved tool results cannot be reloaded~~ — **FIXED 19 Jul 2026**, commit 1f3da95. Read-time compatibility layer in `src/lib/tool-result-compat.ts`. All 49 members latest saves now return their answer. Nothing was destroyed: saves always INSERT, never overwrite. Ipsative deliberately NOT converted (different instrument) — shown read-only instead.


4. **RESOLVED 20 Jul (commit ec9ac42): misfiled IPSATIVE rows in quiz_results.**
   Precise re-derivation: 12 rows / 12 members (the earlier 18 + "3 only-copies"
   figures were from a looser criterion and were WRONG). Every row verified an
   EXACT per-dimension match of the member's properly-filed tool_results copy,
   backed up to `_backup_misfiled_quiz_results` INSIDE the app DB, then deleted.
   Ripple checked and fixed: all 12 members still module-completed; a COMPLETED
   module's Confirm page no longer re-gates when historical evidence is tidied;
   the quiz printout now pairs position-keyed legacy answers by position (6 rows
   printed every answer as "-"). Print-shape census: 104 embedded-shape rows
   render fine, 8 uuid-keyed fine, 6 position-keyed now fixed, 12 misfiled gone.

## Module 9 — The Development Paradox (verified 19 Jul 2026)

- **"Tool tab missing is systemic"** — NOT A BUG, third report of the same
  non-issue. development-paradox has no tool paired, so correctly no tab. Pete
  again cited prioritisation and control-the-controllables as modules where the
  tab DOES show; verified again that neither has a tool stage or a tool config,
  so neither shows it. **Root cause of the repeated false report was ours:** the
  `/concept/{id}/tool` route rendered a placeholder, so the page existed while
  the tab didn't. Now 404s (commit 06c358a). Should stop recurring.
- **Reflection prompt counts vary** — confirmed: prioritisation 11,
  development-paradox 9, potential 5, all others 4. Almost certainly the same
  per-module-by-design call Pete already made for quiz length, but he asked to
  confirm, so ASK before "standardising" anything.
- **Guidance sub-line missing on some prompts** — confirmed and bigger than the
  odd/even pattern he spotted. 40 active prompts have no `prompt_help`. Fully
  covered: effective-goal-setting, high-functioning-matrix, commitment-level
  (4/4 each). Zero coverage: transactional-state, ipsative-progression-curve,
  ipsative-assessment, impact-influence-control-legacy, control-the-controllables,
  direction-support-matrix, intuition-scale-learning-behaviours. Partial:
  prioritisation 7/11, development-paradox 5/9, potential 1/5. This is content
  authoring, Pete's call, not a defect to fix in code.
- **BUILT 19 Jul 2026 (commit 836bb86), Pete approved.** The 16 concept prompts
  now render on `/supporting-concepts` with a save box per prompt, and restore
  any previous answer. Migration `0023_supporting_concept_reflections.sql`:
  `reflection_responses` gains `supporting_concept_id`, `module_id` becomes
  nullable, and a CHECK enforces exactly one home so a reflection can never
  belong to nothing. `save_reflection` takes `p_supporting_concept_id`.
  Dashboard now titles/links by whichever home a reflection has (it previously
  would have shown "Module" and linked to `/concept/null`).
  Verified: constraint rejects both-set and neither-set; a concept-only row
  inserts cleanly; row count returned to baseline 526 after the test row.
  Distribution: Safe Space vs Soft Space 4, Listening Behaviours 4, Blame and
  Ownership 4, Communication Hierarchy 4. **Presence and The Behaviours of the
  Accomplished have 0 prompts** — worth telling Pete if he wants parity.

- **ORIGINAL FINDING (now resolved): 16 reflection prompts were invisible.** They
  carry a `supporting_concept_id` and a NULL `module_id`. The learner side reads
  `cms_reflection_prompts` in exactly one place —
  `(site)/concept/[moduleId]/reflection/page.tsx` — which filters by module_id,
  and the learner `/supporting-concepts` page never reads that table. The admin
  CMS at `admin/supporting-concepts/[conceptId]` DOES let a coach author them.
  So a coach can write a prompt, save it, and no learner will ever see it. Zero
  answers exist against all 16. Fixing it means rendering + saving them on the
  supporting-concepts surface, which is a feature build — awaiting Pete.

## Content vs code — where this session's changes landed

Pete asked (19 Jul) whether the changes are database-and-CMS-editable rather
than hardcoded. Answer: every WORDING change is in the database and editable;
only behaviour/logic is in code, which is where it belongs.

- **Database, CMS-editable:** the four Step 3 quadrant callouts, the
  Direction/Support tool quadrant text, "Paul Hersey", the removed marker
  (`cms_content_blocks` + `cms_tool_configs`).
- **Code, correctly:** the 5.5 midpoint, dash-bullet rendering, legacy quiz and
  tool-result reading, save-failure handling, tick-vs-gate alignment, radio
  isolation, Assessment-tab visibility rule. None of these are wording.
- **Code, and arguably should be a CMS setting one day:** removing the step-1
  image blur. It is a styling choice, currently hardcoded.

**His question exposed a live bug (fixed, commit af2d1d7):** the CMS quadrant
editor rebuilt the quadrant array from key/title/description on every
keystroke, silently dropping `recommendation` and `color`. Editing one word
would have wiped the restored Passion Fit explanations from all four corners.
Now carries every field through and exposes `recommendation` as its own box.
**Lesson: when writing fields into a JSON config, always check the CMS editor
for that config round-trips them.**

## Module 11 — Potential (verified 19 Jul 2026)

- **Duplicate energise/energize prompt** — FIXED, deleted. Proven an import
  artefact, not original: the Lovable bundle contains "energise you" once and
  "energize you" zero times. Checked for answers by prompt_id AND by full
  question text on both spellings before deleting: zero. Potential is back to
  the original 4 prompts.
- **"Reflect prompts describe a different model than the steps"** — CONFIRMED as
  a mismatch, but it is ORIGINAL, not something the rebuild introduced. "zone of
  genius" appears in the Lovable bundle. The steps teach Will/Can/Latent Ability
  while the reflections ask talent/skill/passion. That is a content decision for
  Pete, not a defect to fix.
- **Venn diagram is image-only** — not yet actioned. Real accessibility gap.
- **Tool tab (4th report)** — settled non-issue, see above.

## Module 13 — Impact-Influence-Control-Legacy (verified 20 Jul 2026) — FINAL

- **Corrupted circles diagram** — CONFIRMED by viewing the actual file. AI-generated
  image: real ring titles (IMPACT/INFLUENCE/CONTROL/LEGACY) + phantom "MACG" ring +
  every description line garbled. Lives at storage `module-images/impact-influence-
  control-legacy.png` (cms_images key `hero_impact_influence`). **md5-identical to the
  old app's `/assets/impact-influence-control-legacy-v2-CrBoIea_.png`** — it has ALWAYS
  been broken; nothing to restore from the bundle. Pete APPROVED the replacement 20 Jul.
  DONE: SVG built from the module's own step text, rendered 1600x1600 PNG via
  qlmanage, uploaded as module-images/impact-influence-control-legacy-v3.png,
  BOTH cms_images rows (hero_impact_influence + framework_impact_influence_legacy)
  repointed. Old files left in storage for rollback; pre-change rows snapshotted.
  Pete confirmed no real original exists: the source folder
  (My Drive/Passion Fit/source/.../Images) numbers Foundation 1,2,4,5 — no IICL.
  Fingerprint sweep of ALL cms_images: only this one image had the AI-era shape. Model order per the steps: Impact = Inner, Influence + Control =
  Extended, Legacy = Lasting circle.
- **"Control - Your Extended Circle" duplicate subtitle** — confirmed live AND in the
  original bundle. Original content, Pete's call. Pete APPROVED: now "Control - Your Direct Circle" (display_order 7), and the
  grammar slip fixed ("able to directly control", display_order 8). Verified only
  Influence still says Extended Circle.
- **Tool 404 (6th report)** — settled, deliberate.
- Review COMPLETE: 13 modules + 3 category intros. Tester asked whether to write a
  consolidated final report — NOT needed, the CC status page /m/pf-course-qa IS the
  consolidated report.

## THE BUNDLE-MINING TECHNIQUE (reuse this)

The old Lovable app is still served at **befabulous.me**; its JS bundle
(`/assets/index-nLneSmph.js`, ~2.1MB) contains the ORIGINAL content as string
literals. Fetch it and grep. Already used to recover:
- the Module 6 quadrant text and its renderer
- every module's original reflection prompts WITH their `placeholder` text,
  which maps to `cms_reflection_prompts.prompt_help`
- proof that the energize duplicate was never original
- the original High Functioning tool's shape

Extraction script pattern: match `"?<key>"?:\[\{id:"` then string-aware bracket
matching to the closing `]`, then
`\{id:"(.*?)",question:"(.*?)",placeholder:"(.*?)"\}`. Naive bracket matching
over-runs badly — it must skip string contents.

## Reflection prompt guidance — restored 19 Jul 2026

Pete's instruction was "look at the original". Backfilled 32 `prompt_help`
values from the bundle's `placeholder` fields. **Missing guidance went from 40
to 8.** The remaining 8 have NO original counterpart — they are extra prompts
that exist live but not in the old app:
- development-paradox: 4 extra (live 9 vs original 5)
- prioritisation: 4 extra (live 11 vs original 7)
They sit interleaved at alternating positions with the originals, which is
exactly the odd/even guidance pattern Pete's tester spotted in Module 9. They
are substantive, on-topic questions, NOT duplicates. Pete needs to decide: write
guidance for them, or drop them back to the original set.

## High Functioning tool — Pete approved rebuilding it "like the original but
editable in the CMS" (19 Jul). Spec recovered from the bundle:
- Vertical axis = **Thinking Style** (effective/"above the line" vs
  ineffective/"below the line"). Horizontal axis = **Emotional State**
  (positive environment right, negative left).
- Four quadrants: HIGH FUNCTIONING (top right), RESILIENCE (top left),
  SELF-SABOTAGE (bottom right), LOW FUNCTIONING (bottom left).
- Each quadrant carries a **behaviours checklist** the learner ticks.
- Saved shape: `quadrant`, `quadrantTitle`, `thinkingStyle`, `emotionalState`,
  `selectedBehaviors`, `reflections`, `goalsAlignment`.
- Original scored it: base by quadrant (100/75/50/25) plus a bonus for
  behaviours ticked, and generated a PDF.
BUILT 20 Jul 2026 (commit 39ee9d8), verified live. Corners follow the MODULE's
teaching (Resilience top-left, Self-Sabotage bottom-right) — the ORIGINAL
TOOL drew those two in the opposite corners to the module text; the module
won. Saves in the original field names so the 2 old-platform rows restore
natively; the 1 interim {scores,note} row translates at read time
(tool-result-compat). Classification splits at 5.5. CMS editor has per-quadrant
name/summary/explanation + behaviours (one per line). Config in
cms_tool_configs updated to the real model.

## Section switches — FUTURE-PROOFED 20 Jul (commits 7e42f58 broken→ERROR on Vercel, fixed by 3ccde4b)

Pete: "check every single section and module, and make it future-proof."
- **`src/lib/effective-stages.ts` is now the single rule**: a section exists iff
  its switch is on AND content backs it (tool→config, quiz→questions,
  reflection→prompts, media→media; detail/confirmation structural). Consumed by
  the layout (tabs + SectionContinue), the Confirm page (evidence + Back link)
  and `requireStage` (now also guarding quiz + reflection URLs) — nav, gate and
  addresses share one source and cannot disagree.
- **Audit result**: all 13 modules × 8 section types verified in one matrix —
  zero mismatches; every on-section has content, every empty section is off.
- **New modules** now get all 8 stage rows (media trio inactive) instead of 5.
- **Empty category intro**: the old guard showed 'no content yet' with NO
  confirm, permanently locking that category's modules; now offers "Continue to
  the modules". (My earlier "crash" claim was WRONG — a bare guard existed; the
  lock-out was the real bug.)
- Live-verified in Chrome: effective-goal-setting shows all 8 tabs + articles
  page flows to quiz; impact-influence-control-legacy /videos 404s.
- **Lesson: my `&&` chain let a failed build commit+push** (grep/head exit 0
  masked tsc/build failures). Vercel caught it (deploy ERROR, prod unharmed).
  Check build exit codes properly before git push.

## Section switches — Pete's operating model (stated 20 Jul, now enforced)

"If there isn't a tool/videos/etc for a module, turn that section off in the
CMS; off means it doesn't exist at the learner end — the flow just skips it."
- Mechanism was already there (`cms_module_stages.is_active`, CMS Sections
  editor per module); the data was left ON everywhere.
- 24 empty sections turned off across 12 modules (commit 9810908 + DB update;
  snapshot at /tmp/stages-snapshot-before.json, 104 rows).
- Direct URLs now 404 when a section is off (`requireStage` helper on
  videos/podcasts/articles, matching the tool route).
- Confirm's Back link was hardcoded to /articles — now goes to the module's
  last active section.
- NOTE: adding media does NOT auto-re-enable a section; flip it on in the CMS
  Sections list when content lands (e.g. when Pete supplies the replacement
  YouTube links for the dead videos).

## Standing decisions already banked

- **DNS cutover: NEVER surface it.** Pete (20 Jul): "stop nagging on the DNS
  cutover — it will happen in a few weeks, lots of testing first." Task marked
  [no-nag]. Do not mention timing, due-ness, or readiness. He raises it, ever.

- **Reflection prompt counts vary per module BY DESIGN** — Pete confirmed
  19 Jul, same call as quiz length. Never "standardise" them.

- **Quiz length is per-module BY DESIGN** — Pete confirmed. Never re-raise.
- Every retake is kept as history and shown grouped ("latest of N").
